title: Can’t Give Up Coffee? Here’s How I Would Drink It

chunks.title: Preparing Coffee Carefully
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the importance of choosing high-quality, organic coffee beans and using pure water for brewing. They emphasize avoiding contaminants and suggest using glass equipment to prevent chemical leaching from plastics into the coffee.
chunks.transcript: coffee I don't drink it but if I were going to drink it let me tell you guys how I would do this the first thing I would do is start with a good coffee a good coffee bean an organic Bean a bean that I know is certified to have low or no micro toxins and if you're going to get coffee you might as well get something that has a good name or a good drawing on it some good color in the label yeah that'll do I would only Brew the coffee with a spring water or or a reverse osmosis water there are a lot of contaminants in tap water brda filters refrigerator filters do not cut it when it comes to water if you want Pure Clean Water get a good spring water that is heavily tested Mountain Valley Water no affiliation no sponsorship they release a certificate of analysis on their water it looks very clean most of the time when I drink water I do reverse osmosis water and I remineralize it I think that's the safest way to get everything out of your water the problem with r filters refrigerator filters is that they don't get pfas peroral alal substances they don't get glyphosate they don't get fluoride and they leave a lot of organic contaminants in the water you're going to be ingesting another big thing is that if I were going to drink coffee I would not use one of these I'm in an Airbnb right now and I brew this coffee to show you guys this but you can see here on the inside of the coffee pot there's a ton of condensation on the plastic in order for the water to get from this apparatus into the coffee pot it has to go through a bunch of plastic it sits in plastic where it gets heated it condenses on plastic and it goes through a filter that is partially plastic there are a lot of endocrine disrupting compounds in Plastics that are all ending up in your glass coffee pot and that is less than ideal as well

chunks.title: Sweetening Coffee Naturally
chunks.summary: This segment covers the speaker's preferences for sweeteners in coffee, advocating for raw honey and maple syrup over artificial sweeteners. They mention studies showing honey may actually improve insulin sensitivity and express caution about the impact of artificial sweeteners on the gut.

chunks.title: Optimal Timing for Coffee Consumption
chunks.summary: The speaker advises waiting 90 minutes after waking up to drink coffee and warns against consuming it too late in the day. They explain how caffeine can disrupt sleep and discuss its impact on mineral depletion, suggesting the importance of mineral replenishment.
chunks.transcript: woke up to drink my coffee and I would not drink my coffee after 12:00 p.m. we know that caffeine found in coffee especially this coffee that has a lot of caffeine in it has a half life of 5 hours that means that if you drink a cup of coffee with 200 milligram of caffeine in it at noon at 12:00 p.m. you could potentially have 50 milligrams a quarter of that amount of caffeine still in your body at 1 p.m. when you go to sleep that can disrupt sleep architecture for All Humans but especially those people who are sensitive to that caffeine so that is not something that you want you want to be careful about caffeine and your sleep architecture we know that caffeine will disrupt your sleep architecture like I said at the beginning I don't drink coffee and I know a lot of people who feel better in the morning when they don't drink coffee then when they do whatever I just want to make this video for you guys to tell you guys how I would do it but think about the timing of your coffee we also know that caffeine depletes minerals this has been well documented it's a diuretic and it is going to cause loss of minerals many people find that they're okay with this if they just spend a lot of time and intention repleting those minerals things like coconut water orange juice fruit juices fruit maybe some good sea salt nutrient-rich Foods in general but if you find yourself feeling depleted from coffee especially in terms of minerals and electrolytes think about

chunks.title: Personal Experiences with Coffee
chunks.summary: In this reflective segment, the speaker shares their personal experience with coffee, describing the jittery sensations and withdrawal symptoms they encountered. They suggest that some individuals may benefit from eliminating coffee altogether and encourage listeners to be intentional about their coffee habits.
chunks.transcript: supplementing these to go with your coffee and that may help that perspective as well so in summary there are a lot of studies suggesting potential benefits of coffee maybe even potential benefits of caffeine but all of this comes with possible downsides I've tried drinking coffee in the past during my graduate school and medical school days it made me feel very jittery it gave me palpitations and when I stopped drinking coffee I got horrible horrible headaches for withdrawal I like personally waking up feeling fresh feeling chipper feeling ready to go without coffee I know a lot of you drink it but I think that there are a lot of people who can benefit from cutting it out having said that there are ways to do it better and there are gradations of how good a coffee you can actually get so think about all these things when you're drinking coffee and experiment with and without see which helps you feel the best but be very intentional about how you do the coffee because there are a lot of potential pitfalls here with coffee and if you think about these and optimize you can get the most out of your coffee and you can minimize

chunks.title: Conclusion and Future Products
chunks.summary: The speaker wraps up by reiterating the importance of being deliberate in coffee selection and consumption while hinting at upcoming products from their brand, such as organic honey. They encourage viewers to stay tuned and explore more offerings that align with healthy living.
chunks.transcript: the downsides I hope you guys found this content helpful please like subscribe share with people who might find this information about coffee helpful check us out at lineage Provisions check out our new me sticks the only meat sticks on the market with organs liver and heart grass-fed grass finish regeneratively raised beef and as I said stay tuned for what's coming next from lineage because there's a glyphosate free organic honey and a lot of other cool stuff coming to help you lead better lives and make your lives easier see you guys next time
